title: Operators and conversions

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific source-linked draft lesson for Operators and conversions; independent technical, source, pedagogy, accessibility, and runnable-example review remains required. canonicalExplanation.summary: Java expressions follow operator grouping, short-circuit rules, numeric promotion, conversion, and evaluation order; assignment converts only after the right-hand expression is evaluated.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: The learner predicts that 5 / 2 is 2 before assignment to a double and changes one operand to double when 2.5 is the required result. Your goal is to trace mixed expressions and guard explicit narrowing or overflow-sensitive operations. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: First source and main; pf state expressions; Primitive types and variables. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language-specific operational model
sections.body: Java expressions follow operator grouping, short-circuit rules, numeric promotion, conversion, and evaluation order; assignment converts only after the right-hand expression is evaluated. This model is applied through the declared Java: Beginner to Intermediate behavior rather than a language-neutral slogan.

sections.heading: Boundary and failure model
sections.body: Integer division truncates, narrowing needs an explicit cast but can lose data, overflow wraps for integer arithmetic, and && or || skip a right operand conditionally. The learner must reject this documented misconception: The destination variable type controls every intermediate operation, and an explicit cast makes any narrowing conversion safe.

includes: Evaluate arithmetic, relational, logical and conditional expressions including promotion and narrowing.
Trace mixed expressions and guard explicit narrowing or overflow-sensitive operations.
excludes: Integer division truncates, narrowing needs an explicit cast but can lose data, overflow wraps for integer arithmetic, and && or || skip a right operand conditionally.

outcomes: Trace mixed expressions and guard explicit narrowing or overflow-sensitive operations.

examples.title: Operators and conversions: direct language case
examples.situation: The learner predicts that 5 / 2 is 2 before assignment to a double and changes one operand to double when 2.5 is the required result.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the concrete Java: Beginner to Intermediate state or tool result: The learner predicts that 5 / 2 is 2 before assignment to a double and changes one operand to double when 2.5 is the required result.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the result with this correction: Trace operand promotion and each intermediate operation before the assignment conversion; a cast requests narrowing but does not prove the value fits.
examples.result: The example meets the atomic outcome because Trace operand promotion and each intermediate operation before the assignment conversion; a cast requests narrowing but does not prove the value fits.

examples.title: Operators and conversions: boundary and transfer case
examples.situation: A bounds guard uses index >= 0 && index < values.length so the array access is evaluated only after both safety checks succeed.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name what changed from the direct case: A bounds guard uses index >= 0 && index < values.length so the array access is evaluated only after both safety checks succeed.

Step 2 — Keep the language boundary explicit: Integer division truncates, narrowing needs an explicit cast but can lose data, overflow wraps for integer arithmetic, and && or || skip a right operand conditionally.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting but incorrect rule: The destination variable type controls every intermediate operation, and an explicit cast makes any narrowing conversion safe.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Trace mixed expressions and guard explicit narrowing or overflow-sensitive operations.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: The destination variable type controls every intermediate operation, and an explicit cast makes any narrowing conversion safe.
misconceptions.correction: Trace operand promotion and each intermediate operation before the assignment conversion; a cast requests narrowing but does not prove the value fits.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Predict both examples and identify the first observable contradiction in this belief: The destination variable type controls every intermediate operation, and an explicit cast makes any narrowing conversion safe.

analogy.example: a control panel whose typed gauges, expressions, switches, and repeating cycles produce observable state can introduce Operators and conversions, after the canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate rule is stated first.
usefulFor: tracing primitive values, operators, decisions, and loop progress
limitations: the panel does not model promotion, overflow, short-circuit evaluation, scope, or JVM representation exactly

recap.summary: You should now be able to trace mixed expressions and guard explicit narrowing or overflow-sensitive operations. Keep this boundary in view: Integer division truncates, narrowing needs an explicit cast but can lose data, overflow wraps for integer arithmetic, and && or || skip a right operand conditionally.
retrievalPrompts: Explain Operators and conversions using canonical Java: Beginner to Intermediate terms and one observable consequence.
Why is this belief incorrect: The destination variable type controls every intermediate operation, and an explicit cast makes any narrowing conversion safe.
Predict this case from memory: A bounds guard uses index >= 0 && index < values.length so the array access is evaluated only after both safety checks succeed.
